basics.name: Junteng Liu
basics.summary: First-year PhD candidate at HKUST NLP Group, researching natural language processing and machine learning. Research interests include LLM Reasoning and Reinforcement Learning, Hallucination in Vision-Language Models, and LLM truthfulness and interpretability.

work.summary: Research intern focusing on large language models and multimodal AI.
Research intern working on vision-language models and hallucination mitigation.
Research intern focused on Chinese language model evaluation and efficient fine-tuning.

education.institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

publications.name: SynLogic: Synthesizing Verifiable Reasoning Data at Scale for Learning Logical Reasoning and Beyond
publications.summary: We propose SynLogic, a framework for synthesizing large-scale verifiable reasoning data to train models on logical reasoning tasks and beyond.

publications.name: On the Perception Bottleneck of VLMs for Chart Understanding
publications.summary: We investigate the perception bottleneck in vision-language models that limits their ability to understand and reason about charts.

publications.name: On the Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane Inside LLMs
publications.summary: We discover a universal truthfulness hyperplane inside LLMs that can be used to control model truthfulness.

publications.name: In-Context Sharpness as Alerts: An Inner Representation Perspective for Hallucination Mitigation
publications.summary: Co-authored work on using in-context sharpness as an early warning system for hallucination detection in LLMs.

publications.name: C-Eval: A Multi-Level Multi-Discipline Chinese Evaluation Suite for Foundation Models
publications.summary: Co-authored comprehensive Chinese evaluation benchmark for foundation models across multiple disciplines and difficulty levels.

publications.name: Composing Parameter-Efficient Modules with Arithmetic Operations
publications.summary: Co-authored work on composing parameter-efficient modules using arithmetic operations for more flexible model adaptation.
